title: The Wall
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 17. Opens on Stratholme's inner wall after the Culling and closes on the south road with the north question newly alive.
openLocation: Stratholme's inner defensive wall above the main gate.
transport: Static witness position on the wall; descent on foot; horseback south out of the city.
immediateObjective: Survive the wall, absorb what Stratholme taught her, and move before the knowledge turns into paralysis she cannot leave.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only.
No full Dalaran severance yet; that belongs to Chapter 19.
No explicit Frostmourne naming or Mal'Ganis reveal.
Do not let analysis soften the Culling or outrun the physical wall-and-road experience.

povKnowsAtOpen: The source plan was real and worked locally, but Stratholme had already pushed too much contaminated grain into ovens, homes, and bodies.
Arthas answered that failure with the Culling and carried it through to the end.
Sylvanas witnessed the failed source attempt before the wall, which means the Scholomance scar is under direct pressure from field evidence instead of argument.
The plague's activation leaves a city-scale residue different from anything Jaina has yet observed.
Arthas came out of the gate with clean hands.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether the source model now points to a single broadcaster, a relay chain, or something else entirely.
Whether Antonidas can still speak to the person she is after Stratholme.
Whether home still means anything usable once she leaves this wall.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No full Dalaran severance yet; that belongs to Chapter 19.
No softening of the Culling into something noble because it prevented a wider turn.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: The shared wall witness welds the crack between Jaina and Sylvanas without either woman naming it.
Antonidas reaches Jaina after Stratholme, but the call ends in distance, not resolution.
Jaina's countermeasure model now points past contaminated grain toward a source or relay that broadcasts the activating frequency.
A Farstrider relay confirms Arthas is sailing north for Northrend with a loyalty-selected force.
The chapter ends with the question alive but unspoken: do they keep riding south, or do they turn north after him?

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If she can understand Stratholme precisely enough, she can still salvage something from it besides ash.
pov.surfaceObjective: Stay upright long enough to turn horror into usable knowledge and then move when motion becomes possible again.
pov.pressureSource: Arthas's clean hands, the city-scale residue, the certainty question opened by Kel'Thuzad and Arthas both, and Sylvanas's silent presence beside her.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina still reaches for explanation as if understanding might reduce the cost already paid.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can let witness, grief, and analysis occupy the same space without letting any one of them erase the others.
pov.forcedChoice: Stay on the wall and keep processing, or let Sylvanas pull her back into movement before the wall becomes the only thing she can do.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina moves from "the countermeasure failed at city scale" to "the countermeasure was aimed at the wrong layer; the true answer lies farther north."
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 19 opens with the source model pointing north and both institutions moving to pull these women in the opposite direction.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Former lover, prince, and terminal example of conviction without friction.
throughPov.povPressure: Jaina reads him from inside the old history rather than from military distance.
throughPov.interactionRule: He is physically gone after the opening image. The chapter works on his absence, the wall, and what his last visible choices mean.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Damaged pair on the edge of becoming something harder to break.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: Shared witness finishes the repair that the source attempt started. The proof is not verbal. It lives in touch, timing, and the missing verification beat afterward.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Keep the weld physical and behavioral, not declarative.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No speeches about trust. No narrator summary that outpaces what Jaina can actually observe.

entries.rule: External only. Read her through posture, silence, touch, and what she chooses to do with the body in front of her.
Practical support at the base of the wall and on the road. She keeps the horses, water, and formation ready without demanding language the chapter does not have room for.
Present only through the sending-stone. The pressure is institutional and personal at once, but the call cannot carry full explanation.
Functional late-beat carrier. She brings the northbound fact that turns aftermath into pursuit.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 19 opens one day south of Stratholme with the north vector already established and both institutions trying to reclaim these women before they turn after it.

factsLockedForChapter19: The wall welded the Scholomance crack shut in practice.
Antonidas reached Jaina after Stratholme, but the line remains unresolved rather than cut.
Jaina's countermeasure model now points past contaminated grain toward a broadcaster or relay source.
Arthas is sailing north for Northrend with men already selected by Stratholme.
Uther rides west for Capital City.
South is only the road out from the wall; north is the real next question.
factsNotYetEarned: Sylvanas's spoken refusal to the Convocation.

scenes.title: The Wall
scenes.sceneFunction: Hold Jaina on the wall long enough for the Culling to become personal, magical, and irreversible rather than merely historical.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The city is dead, the source plan failed by timing rather than bad theory, and Arthas has already come through the gate with clean hands.
scenes.arcPressure: Do not let the wall dissolve into abstract grief. Keep it in stone, smoke, silence, residue, and what the body can still feel.

enrichment.detail: Stratholme is Lordaeron's northernmost and second-most populous city, as well as the religious heart tied to the founding of the Silver Hand. That scale matters here: the wall is not overlooking a nameless ruin but one of the kingdom's great civic and sacred centers, which makes the silence and controlled fires feel even more obscene.

beats.dilemma: Move, think, reach, do something, or stay where the wall left her and admit that understanding the mechanism does not produce a next action here.
beats.decision: She does nothing yet. She stands and keeps taking it in.
beats.valueShift: survived the city -> immobilized above it
newOnPageInformation: The silence after the Culling reads differently from ordinary emptiness because a city this large should still be making human noise.
Jaina's body recognizes the city-scale residue before her mind has turned it into theory.
Sylvanas remains beside her in working silence rather than trying to force speech into the scene.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What, exactly, the residue will teach her.
Whether she can move under her own power once the wall stops being the whole world.
sensoryAnchors: warm parapet stone under both palms
fire eating through buildings block by block instead of one general blaze
a silence made louder by the ordinary city hum no longer being there
beats.transitionIntent: image-focus: the next beat tightens from city-wide absence to one intolerable image.
beats.verifierNotes: Stratholme is already established as a living city turned witnessed atrocity. Keep the wall aftermath anchored in stone, smoke, and silence rather than re-introducing the city from scratch.

beats.emotion: Personal reckoning pushed through one visual detail.
beats.dilemma: Let the clean hands remain a damning image and keep it at that, or follow the image backward into the older pattern that made it possible.
beats.decision: Jaina cannot help following it backward.
beats.valueShift: immobilized above it -> the catastrophe becomes personal history
newOnPageInformation: Arthas's concern with presentation survived intact all the way to atrocity.
The Alterac ruling now reads as a small-scale version of the same fatal gap between speed and judgment.
Jaina's old diagnosis was accurate and useless at once.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether being right about Arthas can ever do more than mark the shape of the damage.
How much of this reading comes from history and how much from the wall.
sensoryAnchors: pale hands catching firelight with no blood on them
the memory of a circlet straightened in a mirror
heat on Jaina's face from a city her science could not save
beats.transitionIntent: analysis-pull: once the prince becomes personal again, the chapter can widen back out into the magical residue he walked through.
beats.verifierNotes: The clean-hands image is a fresh chapter17 wound. Do not treat it as a retroactive longstanding Arthas trait; the shock here is Jaina's interior read of a newly witnessed image.

beats.dilemma: Stay inside the human horror of the wall, or admit that her mind is already turning the same wall into data whether she wants it to or not.
beats.decision: She does not choose analysis so much as fail to stop it.
beats.valueShift: personal history reopened -> city-scale magical read
newOnPageInformation: Stratholme gives Jaina the first true city-scale activation read she has ever had.
The contamination's behavior at scale differs from village and laboratory models.
The countermeasure was not false; it was built for smaller, cleaner problems than this city.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the broader source model actually is.
Whether the new information will matter soon enough to save anyone else.
sensoryAnchors: bread-taste wrongness in the back of her throat
the residue fading like a fire that already spent its fuel
smoke, ash, and necromantic drag layered into one air
beats.transitionIntent: mirror-pressure: from magical residue the chapter can move into the harder inward question of what conviction looks like when it outruns friction.

scenes.title: The Diagnosis
scenes.sceneFunction: Push Jaina from "Arthas failed" to the uglier question of what conviction looks like when she shares its structure.
castInScene: Arthas and Kel'Thuzad as mental reference points only
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Stratholme proved the city-scale failure of the existing plan and gave Jaina new data anyway.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep the thought grounded in specific memories and observed consequences rather than free-floating thematic essay.

beats.dilemma: Keep the Alterac ruling filed as a past relationship wound, or admit it was early evidence of the exact thing now burning below.
beats.decision: She admits the line from then to now.
beats.valueShift: city-scale magical read -> old diagnosis becomes indictment
newOnPageInformation: Jaina saw the pattern years earlier in a context small enough to dismiss.
Leaving Arthas solved the relationship but changed nothing about his underlying trajectory.
The wall turns a remembered argument into a mass grave's receipt.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether staying would actually have changed him.
Whether that question matters anymore.
sensoryAnchors: white knuckles on the parapet
heat coming through stone from the fires below
the remembered stillness of a prince who already thought the decision finished
beats.transitionIntent: three-way-mirror: the memory now widens into the comparison she does not want and cannot avoid.
beats.verifierNotes: Alterac and Arthas's older pattern are established history, not a fresh revelation. The beat should deepen Jaina's indictment, not restage the breakup as new information.

beats.emotion: Existential doubt rather than resolved guilt.
beats.dilemma: Treat Kel'Thuzad, Arthas, and herself as three separate moral cases, or face the shared engine underneath them: certainty outrunning friction.
beats.decision: She cannot dismiss the shared engine, even if she refuses the moral equivalence.
beats.valueShift: old diagnosis becomes indictment -> certainty itself becomes suspect
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad removed ethical friction in the name of understanding.
Arthas removed deliberative friction in the name of duty.
Jaina sees a version of the same structure in her own habit of trusting correct analysis to justify continued forward motion.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What separates her from them in practice rather than intent.
Whether Stratholme just gave her the answer in the form of the woman standing beside her.
sensoryAnchors: bread-taste lingering beneath smoke
Kel'Thuzad's chalk remembered in the hand that no longer holds it
Sylvanas's unbroken stillness at Jaina's left shoulder
beats.transitionIntent: touch-and-motion: the chapter has gone as far inward as it can. The only way out now is physical movement.
beats.verifierNotes: Kel'Thuzad as Jaina's methodological mirror is already proven in chapter12. Treat this as escalation of an established self-diagnosis, not as the first time she sees the parallel.

scenes.title: The Anchor
scenes.sceneFunction: Break the paralysis physically and let Jaina observe the partnership change through movement rather than confession.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The wall has already given Jaina more thought than she can use here.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep the wall, stair, shoulder, horse, and formation physically live. Let bodies and objects carry the emotional turn.

enrichment.detail: Farstrider field competence matters in tiny practical ways here: Cyndia has tack, water, and horses ready before either principal asks, and the re-formed riding line carries the same escort logic the trio used on the head-start run into Stratholme. That continuity makes the repaired partnership visible as formation behavior rather than speech.

beats.conflict: Jaina is still standing in the same place hours later, and anything shaped like comfort would break her instead of moving her.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets her moving, but nothing about the movement resolves what the wall opened.

beats.valueShift: certainty itself becomes suspect -> movement becomes possible
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas waits until touch can work before using it.
The hand on Jaina's shoulder functions as direction, not consolation.
"South. We go south." is both route instruction and temporary permission to stop staring.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether south is only escape or the actual next path.
How much of the wall Jaina is carrying with her now that she can move.
sensoryAnchors: leather gauntlet on Jaina's left shoulder
heat still trapped in the parapet stone as she turns away from it
smoke caught in both their clothes
beats.transitionIntent: formation-rebuild: once movement starts, the chapter can show what changed between them on the way down.

beats.goal: Leave the wall and re-form the three-rider line below it.
beats.conflict: The body can move mechanically before the mind catches up, but Jaina notices too much not to take one more measurement on the way down.

outcome.summary: They get off the wall, and Jaina sees the change clearly: the old verification beat is gone. The cost of that clarity is still Stratholme.

beats.valueShift: movement becomes possible -> the crack welds shut in practice
newOnPageInformation: Cyndia has horses, water, and tack ready without asking for language from either of them.
Jaina rides again in the same left-rear position from the head-start run into Stratholme.
Sylvanas no longer inserts the fractional pause that followed Scholomance before acting on Jaina's lane.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What they will do with the welded partnership once the road stops being pure aftermath.
Whether Jaina can name the change before the next crisis uses it.
sensoryAnchors: worn watch-steps under boots
Sylvanas's back below her on the stair, giving the eye somewhere to fix
horse tack already checked and waiting at the base
beats.transitionIntent: outside-interruption: once the formation exists again, the road can start doing travel work and the outside world can finally interrupt the wall.
beats.verifierNotes: The old post-Scholomance verification pause is already gone. Do not write it back in as if the crack were still open at this stage.

scenes.title: The Road and the Pivot
scenes.sceneFunction: Carry the trio out of Stratholme, keep the Antonidas line unresolved but alive, evolve the source model, and let north enter the story as the next real direction.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The crack is practically welded, but nothing else about Stratholme is resolved.
scenes.arcPressure: Travel texture matters here: empty road, smoke behind them, horses under tired bodies, south as immediate route rather than destination.

enrichment.detail: Antonidas is not merely a worried mentor on the stone; canon places him as Grand Magus and head of the Kirin Tor, which makes his extraction offer institutional as well as paternal. The northward pull also matters materially: the northern coast and Northrend route mean colder salt wind arrives before the water is visible, so the road itself can start feeling like a turn toward another climate and another scale of threat.

beats.goal: Answer Antonidas without lying about what the wall did to her.
beats.conflict: The sending-stone can carry safety questions and extraction offers, but not the actual size of Stratholme or the person who came off the wall.

outcome.summary: Antonidas reaches her, but the call ends in distance rather than solution.

beats.valueShift: the crack welds shut in practice -> one old tether still holds, thinly
newOnPageInformation: Antonidas already has fragmentary word of Stratholme.
He can offer extraction immediately if she asks for it.
Jaina can only give him alive and not yet, because the north answer has not finished forming.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina will eventually go back through that line or close it herself.
Whether Antonidas will still sound like home once the next choice is made.
sensoryAnchors: sending-stone warmth in her palm after the call ends
smoke column still visible behind the riders while she speaks
saddlebag weight once the stone goes back in beside notes and samples
beats.transitionIntent: theory-pull: the call leaves the line open; the next beat has to show what knowledge is already pulling past it.
beats.verifierNotes: Antonidas is an established mentor tether, not a surprise restoration or generic recall voice. The beat works only if the pressure comes from distance and insufficiency, not from novelty.

beats.emotion: Redirected purpose rising inside unspent grief.
beats.dilemma: Keep treating the failed countermeasure as a city-scale defeat, or accept that Stratholme may have taught her the real target by proving grain is only the echo.
beats.decision: Jaina follows the thought through to its next shape: find the broadcaster, not the loaves.
beats.valueShift: one old tether still holds, thinly -> failure becomes a north-pointing model
newOnPageInformation: Grain is not the true instrument of the plague; it amplifies a frequency coming from somewhere else.
City-scale activation behaves like a network responding to a broadcast, not a simple chain reaction.
Kel'Thuzad's death removed one relay, not the whole system.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What object, place, or being is broadcasting locally now.
Whether the northbound force is hunting the source, serving it, or both.
sensoryAnchors: frequency carried in the body like a melody she cannot stop hearing
bread-taste fading with distance while the remembered pattern stays sharp
notes in her lap while the horse keeps moving under them
beats.transitionIntent: external-confirmation: once the model points beyond grain, the outside world can arrive and name where the next road goes.
beats.verifierNotes: The broadcaster-or-relay model is a new inference built from established plague mechanics. Do not re-explain the grain-carrier logic as if that older part of the theorem were newly discovered here.

beats.goal: Receive the first actionable intelligence after Stratholme that is not about the city behind them.
beats.conflict: A Farstrider relay rider catches them at dusk with facts that are simple and terrible: Arthas is not riding home. He is taking ships north.

outcome.summary: They finally get the next real direction, but it points off the map they have been living on.

beats.valueShift: failure becomes a north-pointing model -> the prince and the source converge on the same destination
newOnPageInformation: Arthas is commandeering ships for Northrend.
Uther rides west to warn Terenas.
The force sailing north consists of men who stayed through Stratholme and proved they will follow him past moral collapse.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether pursuit is possible with the resources they still have.
Whether north means source, trap, or both.
sensoryAnchors: relay horse lathered at the chest
dispatch tube slapped into a waiting hand
dusk road wind starting to cool with open water somewhere ahead
beats.transitionIntent: live-question: the chapter can end only on the pressure created by this shared destination.

beats.dilemma: Keep riding south because south is the current road, or admit that every meaningful thread now pulls north whether or not either woman says the word yet.
beats.decision: No spoken decision yet. They keep riding, carrying the question into camp and into the next chapter.
beats.valueShift: the prince and the source converge on the same destination -> pursuit becomes imaginable
newOnPageInformation: The chapter ends with south as immediate motion and north as actual pressure.
Jaina's revised model and the relay report now point in the same direction.
The next chapter can turn from aftermath into choice.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who will say north first.
What each woman is willing to lose to mean it.
sensoryAnchors: evening road under hooves
Stratholme's smoke flattening into a stain on the horizon behind them
the sending-stone still faintly warm in the saddlebag while the north answer forms around it
beats.transitionIntent: chapter19-opener: one day later the question becomes operationalized in camp, on the map, and under institutional pressure.

warnings: Beat headings implied scene/sequel types, but the scaffold lacked explicit `beatType` / `scenePhase` fields; both were normalized in this plan output.

Beat 7 was labeled `Scene/Sequel` in the outline; normalized to `scene` because the beat's contract is goal / conflict / outcome, but keep its reflective texture on the stair so it does not read like pure transit.
Meta info referenced a non-existent `stories/the-plague-road/locations/stratholme.md` and an outdated Antonidas path in `supporting.md`; normalized to valid references.

Beats 1-5 are five consecutive sequel/internal beats. The opening still works because it starts mid-sensation, but the draft needs hard stone / smoke / residue anchoring so the chapter does not stall in abstraction.
All four scene beats (6, 7, 8, 10) land `yes-but`, so outcome variety is narrow. Differentiate the costs sharply: motion, weld, tether, and northward shock are not the same compromise.

Value shifts darken in one general direction from beats 1-5 before the road starts converting shock into movement and direction. Keep each early turn distinct — silence, clean hands, residue, indictment, and shared-engine doubt.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter17's wall witness: the Culling has finished, Jaina, Sylvanas, and Cyndia are still on Stratholme's inner wall, and Arthas has just looked up beneath them with washed hands before walking away. Chapter18 opens in the stunned stillness after he leaves, with the city's silence, the proved-too-late source plan, and the clean-hands image all still physically present.

craftResearch.findings: Let silence, bodily reaction, and concrete physical detail carry the weight after violence; under-explaining lands harder than melodramatic over-processing.
craftResearch.source: Writer's Digest on difficult scenes; general aftermath-scene craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: Reflective beats should be triggered by present sensory prompts and steer back toward a decision or movement, not float free as essay.
craftResearch.source: BubbleCow scene craft guidance; Alive Ventures scene-detail guidance

craftResearch.findings: Skip dead-air travel, keep only meaningful movement, and carry emotional continuity forward through sensory anchors and unresolved pressure.
craftResearch.source: R.M. Archer on scene transitions; James Field on time and distance in narrative

craftResearch.findings: Aftermath and sequel beats should end on a decision, fresh vector, or unresolved pressure rather than on explanation or emotional closure.
craftResearch.source: Jane Friedman on scene fundamentals; Linda S. Clare on tension in sequel scenes

craftResearch.findings: Re-establish time, place, and emotional carry quickly after a shift so movement feels continuous rather than like teleportation.
craftResearch.source: R.M. Archer transition patterns; James Field on narrative continuity

pacing.notes: The opening five-beat inward run is risky on paper but survives because each beat changes the frame: silence, clean hands, residue, old diagnosis, and shared-engine doubt all do distinct work before beat 6 turns the chapter bodily. The back half keeps the wall alive through stair, saddle, stone-call, revised model, and northbound relay instead of dropping into inert aftermath.

structure.notes: The opening is strong for an aftermath chapter because it begins in the wall's stunned physical stillness rather than recap. Beat 6 is the clear midchapter turn from inward witness to motion, and beat 11 closes on a live north-versus-south pressure instead of prematurely deciding the next chapter's choice.

character.notes: POV discipline holds throughout: every beat stays inside Jaina's perception, and her agency lives in interpretation, refusal, and choice rather than in spectacle. Sylvanas remains the external anchor, Cyndia restores field function, Antonidas is the thin institutional tether, and the relay rider carries the single late-beat fact without blurring anyone else's role.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified. No beat was missing transitionIntent, the wall-to-diagnosis carry and road-to-relay-to-question carries all land cleanly, and the chapterBridge now explicitly preserves the chapter17 seam without recap.

continuity.notes: 3 new beats, 0 callbacks, 8 evolutions. No contradictions surfaced against memory indexes or targeted files. Anti-reintroduction guards were added for Stratholme as established setting, the clean-hands image as a fresh wound, Alterac as old history, the Kel'Thuzad mirror as chapter12 knowledge, the missing verification pause as already gone, and Antonidas as an existing tether rather than a surprise re-entry.

modifications.description: Added a direct-carry chapterBridge from chapter17 so chapter18 opens on the same wall-state and clean-hands aftermath instead of implying recap or lost time.
Annotated every beat with continuityStatus and added memoryRef objects to all evolution beats, then assembled a focused requiredMemory list for downstream agents.
Added beat-level verifierNotes so the chapter advances established wall, Arthas, Kel'Thuzad, Antonidas, and partnership state rather than re-staging them as fresh revelations.
